selected_topic: The United Kingdom announced a default midnight-to-6 a.m. social-media curfew for 16- and 17-year-olds, although teenagers will be able to override the setting.

topic_selection_result.selection_method: Fixed rubric: sum of 6 fixed scores (TOPIC_SCORING_FIELDS only, max 30). Highest total wins. No manual override.

candidate_1.editorial_interest_hypothesis: A default-but-overridable curfew invites debate about whether opt-out policies are meaningfully protective versus symbolic; the personal stakes (parents/teens/screen time) may give this strong relatability despite being a policy story.

source_refs.paraphrased_headline: Government announces overnight social-media limits and restrictions on addictive features for older teenagers
source_refs.paraphrased_gist: Apps will automatically apply a midnight-to-6 a.m. curfew to 16- and 17-year-olds, while certain engagement features will also be disabled by default.

source_refs.paraphrased_headline: Britain proposes a voluntary six-hour social-media curfew for 16- and 17-year-olds
source_refs.paraphrased_gist: The measure is designed to protect sleep and concentration but can be switched off by users.

source_refs.paraphrased_headline: UK plans an overnight social-media limit for older teenagers
source_refs.paraphrased_gist: The government had not yet named every covered app; the proposal would be a default setting rather than a mandatory ban.

source_refs.paraphrased_headline: Britain plans a default overnight social-media curfew for 16- and 17-year-olds
source_refs.paraphrased_gist: The policy is an extension of Britain's planned under-16 social-media restrictions; additional controls will target features designed to keep users scrolling.

verified_facts: The announcement was made on July 15, 2026.
The target age group is 16- and 17-year-olds.
The default restricted period is midnight to 6 a.m.
The setting can be overridden by the user, so it is not an absolute overnight ban.
The government also plans to disable or limit certain addictive features by default.
The policy follows a government pilot involving more than 300 teenagers and parents.
The announcement is part of a broader British plan to restrict social-media access for children under 16.

script_generation_input.topic: The United Kingdom announced a default midnight-to-6 a.m. social-media curfew for 16- and 17-year-olds, although teenagers will be able to override the setting.
script_generation_input.facts:
VERIFIED FACTS:
- The announcement was made on July 15, 2026.
- The target age group is 16- and 17-year-olds.
- The default restricted period is midnight to 6 a.m.
- The setting can be overridden by the user, so it is not an absolute overnight ban.
- The government also plans to disable or limit certain addictive features by default.
- The policy follows a government pilot involving more than 300 teenagers and parents.
- The announcement is part of a broader British plan to restrict social-media access for children under 16.

GENERAL KNOWLEDGE CONTEXT (background only, do not present as breaking facts):
- Concerns about teenagers' sleep, concentration, and social-media use have been widely discussed by parents, educators, and researchers in recent years.
